content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom indices labeled. Let me focus on atom C:28 (carbon with index 28) and identify its neighboring atoms.

From the image, I can clearly see that atom C:28 is connected to:
- C:29 (carbon, index 29) - to the upper right
- C:27 (carbon, index 27) - to the lower left
- H:33 (hydrogen, index 33) - to the right
- H:31 (hydrogen, index 31) - to the upper left

Looking at the connections more carefully:
- C:28 is bonded to C:29 (visible bond)
- C:28 is bonded to C:27 (visible bond)
- C:28 is bonded to H:33 (visible bond)
- C:28 is bonded to H:31 (visible bond)

All four bonds are clearly visible in this view. Carbon atom 28 has four covalent bonds as expected for a saturated carbon atom.

Regarding the geomconf_index, from the file name "geomconf_3401.xyz", I can infer that the geomconf_index is 3401.

So the neighbor atoms of atom C:28 are:
- Atom index 29, element C
- Atom index 27, element C
- Atom index 33, element H
- Atom index 31, element H

And the geomconf_index is 3401.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 28, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
